stancer 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Get started

    Detect stance using COLA framework

    llm_stance.Rd

    llm_stance() implements a multi-stage "Collaborative rOle-infused LLM-based Agents" (COLA) framework to detect stance in text towards a specific target or statement. It supports character vectors and data frames (compatible with mall style).

    The character method performs the full three-stage analysis:

    1. Expert Analysis: Linguistic, domain, and social media perspectives.

    2. Stance Debates: Parallel generation of arguments for, against, and neutral.

    3. Judgement: Final synthesis into a structured stance score.

    llm_stance(x, ...)

# S3 method for class 'character'
llm_stance(
  x,
  target,
  chat_base,
  type = c("object"),
  language = stancer_available_languages(),
  scale = c("categorical", "numeric", "likert"),
  domain_role = NULL,
  prompts_dir = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE,
  rpm = 20,
  ...
)

# S3 method for class 'data.frame'
llm_stance(
  x,
  text = NULL,
  target = NULL,
  type = NULL,
  domain_role = NULL,
  language = NULL,
  chat_base,
  scale = "categorical",
  prompts_dir = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE,
  rpm = 20,
  .output_col = ".stance",
  ...
)

    Arguments

    x

    Input text(s) as a character vector or a data.frame containing (any of) text, target, type, domain_role or language arguments.

    ...

    Additional arguments passed to ellmer::parallel_chat_structured.

    target

    Character. The entity or statement the stance is directed at.

    chat_base

    An ellmer::Chat object or a list of up to 3 such objects.

    type

    Analysis type: "object" (entity-based) or "statement" (proposition-based).

    language

    Language code (e.g., "en", "ru"). If NULL, auto-detected.

    scale

    Stance scale: "categorical", "numeric", or "likert".

    domain_role

    Optional specific role for the domain expert (e.g., "historian").

    prompts_dir

    Optional path to custom prompt templates.

    verbose

    Logical. Show progress and summary table.

    rpm

    Integer. Global requests per minute limit.

    text

    The text column (if x is a data.frame) or character vector to analyse.

    .output_col

    Name of the column where results will be stored.

    Value

    A stance_result object (for character method) or a modified data frame (for data.frame method).

    S3 object of class "stance_result" with:

    • summary: data.frame with results

    • analysis: expert analysis results

    • debates: debate results

    • judgements: final judgements

    • metadata: processing metadata ⁠@importFrom ellmer is_chat

    Details

    Chat Configuration Recommendations

    For optimal results, consider the following requirements for each stage:

    • Expert Analysis (Stage 1): Requires excellent real-world awareness and reasoning. Structured output is NOT required.

    • Stance Debates (Stage 2): Requires creativity and strong writing skills. Structured output is NOT required.

    • Stance Judgement (Stage 3): Requires large context width, superior reasoning, and MUST support structured output (JSON mode).

    Examples

    # result <- llm_stance(
#   x = "I support renewable energy",
#   target = "renewable energy",
#   chat_base = chat_model,
#   type = "object",
#   scale = "categorical"
# )
# results <- llm_stance(
#   x = tweets_df,
#   text = "text",
#   target = "climate_change",
#   chat_base = chat_model
# )
